explanation: This LTL formula captures the requirement that exceeding sensor limits (!good) while the system is supported (supported) and the pilot is not in standby (!standby) will eventually lead to the autopilot pulling up (F pullup). The use of the "until" operator ensures that the pullup action is taken as long as the conditions (!good & supported & !standby) remain true.  It does not assume that the "pullup" state is necessarily maintained after the fault condition resolves, and it correctly accounts for the need for the system to be supported and the pilot not to be in control in order to trigger the action.

This LTL formula captures the requirement that if the sensor limits are exceeded (!good), and the pilot is not in control (!standby), and the system is supported (supported), then eventually the autopilot will pull up (F pullup).  It uses the until operator (U) to express that the pullup must occur as long as the conditions (!good & !standby & supported) remain true.

This LTL property states that if the sensor limits are exceeded (!good), and the pilot is not in control (!standby), and the system is supported (supported), and there is no autopilot failure (!apfail), then eventually the autopilot will pull up (F pullup). This captures the input natural language by expressing the condition under which the autopilot pull-up should occur and ensuring that it happens eventually.  There are no previous LTL properties to compare against.
